The 3D Avatar (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 2
Speaker 1: All right. Welcome back to lesson two of module two. This one is all about the three
dimensional ever-evolving avatar. In the last lesson, we learned that our prospect much
be engaged on this transformative journey between point A and point Buyer.
And we also saw that there are seven major milestones as well as three state checks to
move them along, to move them along in a reaffirming way. And that this journey all
starts with validation, this recognition of where they're currently at and culminates with
this powerful sense of vindication, this sense of being exonerated from all past actions
and fears and inaction as it relates to the problem that they're trying to solve.
So these are all some very strong human emotions, which of course, if we're going to do
them successfully require well, a living, breathing human. You know, we've got to make
it alive and I know that a lot of you have already been doing some work with empathy
mapping and customer personas and these things aren't necessary unfamiliar to you.
And I want to just mention that what we're doing here isn't meant to detract to any of
that at all. What we're doing isn't contradictory but actually complimentary. And the
same goes for the whole quantitative versus qualitative debate. You know, there are
some amazing lessons in TenX emails and other blog posts throughout Copy Hackers
that teach you how to gather data through surveys and other types of data mining
methods.
And that's all amazing. We're not discounting from that at all. What we're doing here, is
we're simply breathing extra life. We're drilling deeper into it, going deeper into the
surface and generating the ahas and insights that won't show up necessarily on a
spreadsheet, a customer response or a standard empathy map.
So what we're doing is, it's about taking these personas, these data points, these avatars
that you have created and actually making them real for you. Now empathy mapping is
a great start but how many of us have been guilty of doing the exercise it wants,
creating this two-dimensional avatar with a stock image of some guy named Tony and
then having it hibernate in a Google doc forever?
You know, cold weathered, starving to death in some kind of pixelated health. So that's
where we're moving away from. Obviously two-dimensional Tony isn't very useful to us
when we're trying to create messaging for a funnel that connects on those deepest
levels and inspires a very real, and I keep using and emphasizing the word real
transformation for very real people.
And this is something that I tend to have hanging on my office bulletin board. Every time
I start slipping into that, that sense of my customer not being real, you know. If it
bleeds, we can sell it. That is, that is Predator. I think it was Arnold or Carl Weathers,
you know one of them that dropped the line in that movie when the first discovered
that this thing, this creature could bleed.
If it bleeds, we can kill it, I think they said. But if it bleeds we can sell it so it's our job to
make it our avatar three dimensional, to fill it in with flesh, blood, bones, guts and a
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

The 3D Avatar (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 2
heartbeat that fuels the journey. And this is one thing that is said in a workshop in
Sanoma to one of our Mastermind groups. and it was totally spontaneously at the
moment when I was on the fly.
I am packing my own writing process and what I go through on a very visceral level
when writing for, not even my own audience, but my client's audiences. I simply said I
don't write until I feel like expletive. You know, and until I've so deeply embodied and
identified with the person I'm talking to and until I truly feel empathy not as a loaded
buzz word, but my own lived realty. When I'm so desperate to solve their problems
because their problems and pain have become my own.
And you know, that's become that's were the best messaging comes from. And it may
sound esoteric and inaccessible, but the exercise that I'm going to lead you in later in
this lesson will actually start to ground this, and something actually you can use right
away. And I suggest you do these things before starting any campaign especially if
you're a copywriter working for clients and have to identify with an audience as quickly
and effectively as possible.
Because at the end of the day. empathy is one the greatest gifts you can give another
human, but it doesn't belong on a map. It's real. It's accessible. And to the greater
degree that you choose, that you voluntarily choose to tap into it, and then express it on
the page, the greater you'll connect. The greater your ability to convert, and the greater
you will be as a conversion coach.
So, deep breaths. I have two exercises waiting for you in the next lesson. Get something
warm. Get a good coffee. Get the blood flowing, and I will meet you there at the next
lesson in just a minute.
